channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Five key takeaways from the NATO summit in Ankara
item.description: Trump dominates summit with anti-Europe and Iran diatribes as NATO pledges to expand defence budgets and Ukraine support

item.title: Argentina fans accused of violence, racism by Egypt, Cape Verde supporters
item.description: Fans tell Al Jazeera Argentina supporters hurled beer bottles and abuse at them when their team scored in knockout games.

item.title: Mass cleanup underway after severe flooding in China
item.description: Residents and volunteers in China’s Guangxi province have begun clearing up their flooded homes.

item.title: Trump says US ‘may take over Kharg Island’: Here’s what you need to know
item.description: Kharg Island, pivotal to Iran's oil exports, becomes focal point as Trump renews threats amid rising Gulf tensions.

item.title: War on Gaza draws large crowds to Khamenei’s funeral in Iraq
item.description: More than a million mourners packed streets in Najaf, Iraq for Iranian Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei’s funeral procession.

item.title: Israeli air strikes in Gaza kill eight, including two children
item.description: Medics report 12 injuries alongside eight deaths in Gaza as Israeli air strikes target civilians and displaced families.

item.title: Trump says he ‘may be gone’ as he declares himself Iran’s No. 1 target
item.description: US President Donald Trump declared himself Iran’s ‘No. 1 target’ after claiming their leaders have been eliminated

item.title: Spanish football legend Alexia Putellas joins London City Lionesses
item.description: Women's Super League club lands the two-time Ballon d'Or winner after her exit from Barcelona following a 14-year career.

item.title: France returns Syrian artefacts it’s held since civil war
item.description: France has returned 23 Syrian antique artefacts it’s held since the outbreak of the 2011 civil war.

item.title: How Morocco can win the FIFA World Cup 2026 title
item.description: Morocco has long dreamed of a World Cup title. Now, the trophy is finally within reach - can they seize the moment?

item.title: NATO pledges 70 billion euros for Ukraine as Trump praises peace ‘progress’
item.description: Trump says US will 'show' Ukraine how to produce Patriot missile systems during a friendly appearance with Zelenskyy.

item.title: The Strait of Hormuz is now at the centre of Iranian and US calculus
item.description: The fate of the strait is directly linked to what happens next to the world economy.

item.title: US charges Indian crime leader in Sikh activist’s assassination
item.description: US indicts Indian crime boss, links him to Sikh leader's killing that strained India-Canada relations.

item.title: Egypt criticises ‘influential refereeing’ in Argentina World Cup match
item.description: Egyptian football officials have reportedly lodged a complaint with FIFA against the referees in their last-16 match.

item.title: Far-right leader Le Pen starts campaign, faces mixed reactions in France
item.description: A court ruling has allowed Le Pen to campaign despite embezzlement conviction, sparking heated debates across France.

item.title: Pakistan military says 42 killed in fighter attacks in Balochistan
item.description: Military spokesman warns fighters 'we will chase you, we will hurt you' amid the spate of deadly ambushes.

item.title: Oil for unity: What is the US mediation plan in Libya?
item.description: The plan, led by Trump relative Massad Boulos, promises oil investments if rival factions work together.

item.title: Germany urges US-Iran negotiations, offers to minesweep Hormuz
item.description: German Foreign Minister Johann Wadephul urged the US and Iran to resume negotiations.

item.title: Trump’s Iran strike comments shake markets, Brent crude hits two-week high
item.description: Travel stocks plunged as fuel costs rise, United Airlines fell 3 percent, while energy stocks saw gains.

item.title: Man sets wheelchair user in flames with Molotov cocktail
item.description: US police have released video showing the moment a man set a wheelchair user on fire.

item.title: FIFA World Cup: Quarterfinal brackets, match schedule and game previews
item.description: Talking points as France, Morocco, Spain, Belgium, Norway, England, Argentina and Switzerland aim for World Cup glory.

item.title: Zelenskyy seeks NATO support as Russia continues attacks
item.description: At the NATO summit, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy had urged European allies to build missiles.

item.title: Why have 100,000 people been signed off work with ADHD in the UK?
item.description: New government statistics find four million people claimed disability benefits in April.

item.title: Trump on Iran: ‘We’ll probably hit them hard again tonight’
item.description: US President Donald Trump says the US will ‘probably’ carry out another round of strikes on Iran on Wednesday night.

item.title: ‘Down with the USA’: US influencer Jackson Hinkle attends Khamenei’s funera
item.description: ‘Down with the USA’: US influencer Jackson Hinkle attends Khamenei’s funeral
